heroHeadline: Hamas: Phase One Obligations Must Be Met Before Disarmament; IDF Strikes Continue as Ceasefire Violations Mount
heroSubtitle: The deadliest attack on Jewish people since the Holocaust — assault timeline, hostage crisis resolved Jan 2026, intelligence failures, and IDF response. On April 23, 2026, Hamas formally stated that insisting on disarmament without implementing Phase One of the ceasefire deal 'contradicts Trump's plan,' deepening the Phase Two negotiation deadlock. The IDF struck terrorist weapons transport in southern Gaza and eliminated multiple operatives near the Yellow Line on April 22-23. An April 22 airstrike in northern Gaza killed at least 5 Palestinians including 3 children, with 786 Palestinians killed since the October 10, 2025 ceasefire. The UN-EU-World Bank $71.4B reconstruction assessment (Apr 21) found human development set back 77 years. Cairo backchannel talks continue with no breakthrough; two Board of Peace disarmament deadlines (Apr 14, Apr 19) have passed without Hamas acceptance.
footerNote: Data sourced from IDF, Israel Police, UN OCHA, Israeli government, Reuters, AP, Haaretz, and institutional analyses. Casualty figures are official Israeli counts; Hamas-theater figures are separately noted and contested. All 251 hostages accounted for as of January 26, 2026. Cumulative conflict dead (Oct 7, 2023 – Apr 2026): 72,265+ Palestinians per OCHA/Gaza MoH. Ceasefire in effect since Oct 2025; 2,400+ violations documented by Gaza GMO as of Apr 23, 2026 — 786 Palestinians killed since ceasefire. Gaza reconstruction cost: $71.4B per UN-EU-World Bank joint assessment (Apr 21, 2026).
